prose:
Method & provenance
Property: Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for formaldehyde; headline value reported is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier "standard"), no density fitting, PySCF backend (version not reported).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz (4 atoms) taken as-is; single-point charge analysis – no geometry optimization (notation: Fukui//input geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet). Cation and anion states were modeled as doublets (±1 charge, multiplicity 2) per default.
Convergence & warnings: integrity.status = "ok"; integrity.trustworthy = true. Engine checks flagged three WARNINGS (quoted verbatim below) but all marked ok. SCF calculations for N, N+1, and N–1 states converged; sum-rule tests passed exactly (=1.0000) indicating charge conservation.
Live log & files: the run streamed a live PySCF/chemkit log to the default `fukui_<timestamp>.out` file in the working directory; the complete JSON result is in `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpnla12f8c/live.json`, and the bar-chart PNG is at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_o3/20260704-153604_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.

Key results (Mulliken-based indices, per-atom order O C H H):
• f⁺ = [0.46814, 0.70839, –0.088269, –0.088260]
• f⁻ = [0.46742, 0.11244, 0.21008, 0.21006]
• Dual descriptor Δf = [ 0.000714, 0.59595, –0.29835, –0.29832 ]
Most electrophilic site: atom 2 (C) with f⁺ = 0.7083913.
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.46742464.
